findings: The hook uses a concrete garden-area scenario that gives students an immediate, visible reason to expand (x + 3)(x + 5) before any exponent or FOIL terminology appears.
The exponent-law table presents all five rules with a worked numeric example next to each rule, so a student can verify the rule and apply it in the same glance.
The classify slide separates 'how many terms' from 'what is the degree' into two cards and makes the named families - monomial, binomial, trinomial - obvious instead of buried in narration.
Both pause problems are solvable from material already shown, and each is followed by a visually distinct answer-reveal slide with the full algebra rather than just the final answer.

The (x + 3)^2 trap slide directly targets the most common Algebra I mistake of distributing the exponent term-by-term, with a side-by-side wrong / right column showing the 6 x cross-product that students drop.
The closing path slide gives students concrete Learn Portal next actions (OpenStax reading, Khan Academy practice, dashboard assignment, advisor check-in) rather than generic encouragement.
watch_points: The exponent-law table on slide 5 packs five laws into one frame; narration must move slowly here and the contact-sheet thumbnail should be re-checked at small size.
Students who still think x^2 * x^3 = x^6 (multiplying instead of adding exponents) may need an advisor follow-up before Module 13 - factoring leans heavily on reversing the product law.
